title: Apply P-029: protected-paths guardrail (guard_paths.py + PreToolUse[Bash] hook + state.json key)
description: HUMAN GATE APPROVED (operator, 2026-07-06, via coordinator session): implement P-029 exactly as specified in .harness/logs/audit_t303_protected_paths.md mutation section. Scope: (1) new .harness/bin/guard_paths.py PreToolUse hook script — reads protected_paths glob list from state.json, denies Bash commands whose remove/move/shred/truncate targets or shell redirects match a protected glob, logs a protected_path_blocked event to events.jsonl, fail-open on internal error like log_event.py; (2) wire PreToolUse[Bash] in .claude/settings.json; (3) add protected_paths: [] key to state.json (STRICT default-empty no-op); (4) one-line note in CLAUDE.md §3A is NOT in scope for the worker (NLAH file — coordinator applies gated text separately if needed; confine yourself to the three code/config targets). Mechanical acceptance: with protected_paths=[] every existing scratch cleanup command passes (no-op proven); with a test glob (e.g. .harness/tests_protected/**) a destructive command against it is denied and the event appears in events.jsonl; a destructive command against /private/tmp scratch still passes.

notes.note: plan: write guard_paths.py PreToolUse[Bash] hook (destructive verb/redirect target vs state.json protected_paths glob, deny+log protected_path_blocked, fail-open), wire into .claude/settings.json PreToolUse[Bash], add protected_paths: [] to state.json; test no-op/deny/restore per T-310 acceptance

HANDOFF -> verifier: T-310 (P-029 protected-paths guardrail) — implementation + replay evidence.

FILES CHANGED
- .harness/bin/guard_paths.py (new): PreToolUse[Bash] hook. Fail-open on any
  internal error (try/except -> exit 0), fast no-op when tool_name != "Bash",
  when tool_input has no "command", or when state.json protected_paths is
  empty/absent. Otherwise extracts destructive-verb (rm|mv|shred|truncate)
  arguments (shlex-tokenized so quoted paths with spaces stay one token,
  falling back to naive split on ValueError) and redirect (>/>>) targets,
  resolves each against every protected_paths glob both as-given and as
  resolved-relative-to-repo-root (fnmatch), and on first match: appends a
  protected_path_blocked event via harness_common.log_event (command
  truncated to 400 chars, target, matched glob) then writes a check_lock.py
  -style human-readable reason to stderr and exits 2. No match -> exit 0.
- .claude/settings.json: added a second PreToolUse entry, matcher "Bash",
  running `python3 "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.harness/bin/guard_paths.py"`
  (timeout 10), left alongside (not replacing) the existing
  Edit|Write|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit -> check_lock.py entry and all other
  hooks (SessionStart/PostToolUse/Stop) untouched.
- .harness/state.json: added top-level "protected_paths": [] (sibling to
  "limits"/"human_gates"/"cost_policy"), mutated via harness_common.guarded()
  read-modify-write + atomic_write_json (flock-serialized, same convention
  every other state.json writer uses) while holding the lock.py file lock.
- CLAUDE.md: NOT touched (explicitly out of scope per task text).

PROTOCOL NOTES
- Claimed via `blackboard.py claim T-310 --agent worker-c`.
- check_lock.py initially refused my own Write to guard_paths.py because the
  ambient session identity is "main" while the lock holder was "worker-c";
  resolved via the sanctioned P-002 path: `session.py register worker-c
  --task T-310` (event session_holder_registered in events.jsonl,
  2026-07-06T16:27:08Z), after which the Write succeeded.
- Locks acquired on all three edited files via lock.py before any edit;
  released after (see below).

MECHANICAL TEST REPLAY (exact commands + observed results)

(1) No-op proof, protected_paths=[] (shipped default), destructive command
    against a /private/tmp scratch path:
    mkdir -p /private/tmp/guard_paths_test && touch /private/tmp/guard_paths_test/scratch.txt
    echo '{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"rm -f /private/tmp/guard_paths_test/scratch.txt"}}' \
      | python3 .harness/bin/guard_paths.py
    -> observed: exit 0, file still allowed to be removed by the real shell
       (guard_paths.py does not itself run the command; Claude Code does,
       only after a hook exit-0). No protected_path_blocked event emitted.

(2) Test glob set + deny + event proof. Set protected_paths to a temp glob
    via the same locked guarded()/atomic_write_json technique used for the
    real key add:
      state["protected_paths"] = [".harness/tests_protected/**"]
    mkdir -p .harness/tests_protected && touch .harness/tests_protected/dummy.txt

    2a. Relative-path destructive command (synthetic stdin, same technique
        as the T-004/T-011 check_lock smoke tests):
        echo '{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"rm -f .harness/tests_protected/dummy.txt"}}' \
          | python3 .harness/bin/guard_paths.py
        -> observed: exit 2, stderr "HARNESS PROTECTED PATH: command targets
           '.harness/tests_protected/dummy.txt', which matches protected
           glob '.harness/tests_protected/**' ...".
        events.jsonl gained: {"event":"protected_path_blocked","agent":"main",
          "command":"rm -f .harness/tests_protected/dummy.txt",
          "target":".harness/tests_protected/dummy.txt",
          "glob":".harness/tests_protected/**"}

    2b. Absolute, space-containing, quoted-path destructive command (payload
        written to a scratch file via the Write tool to avoid the outer dcg
        guard/my own real Bash call matching the embedded literal text):
        rm -f "/Users/mariocasanova10pa/Documents/Universal Harness/.harness/tests_protected/dummy.txt"
        -> observed: exit 2, target resolved+matched via the
           resolve()+relative_to(root) fallback path in _target_matches.
        (This surfaced and fixed a real bug: the initial naive
        args_str.split() broke the quoted absolute path — this repo's own
        directory name contains a space — into two tokens and missed the
        match; fixed by tokenizing with shlex.split() instead, with a
        fallback to naive split on ValueError.)

    2c. Redirect form:
        echo hi >> .harness/tests_protected/dummy.txt
        -> observed: exit 2, target ".harness/tests_protected/dummy.txt"
           matched via REDIRECT_RE.

    2d. Legitimate /private/tmp scratch command still passes while the test
        glob is active (selectivity / no false positive):
        echo '{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"rm -f /private/tmp/guard_paths_test/scratch.txt"}}' \
          | python3 .harness/bin/guard_paths.py
        -> observed: exit 0.

    grep -c protected_path_blocked .harness/logs/events.jsonl -> 5 after
    2a/2b/2c (3 new denies logged on top of pre-existing 2 that predate this
    task's live-session incident below).

    INCIDENTAL LIVE PROOF (not a scripted step, but real corroboration): once
    .claude/settings.json was wired, MY OWN Claude Code session's real Bash
    tool calls started routing through the live guard_paths.py. Two of my own
    Bash calls whose literal command TEXT happened to contain a destructive
    verb/redirect pointing at the then-active test glob were denied by the
    actual PreToolUse hook (not just the synthetic-stdin harness) — visible
    as "PreToolUse:Bash hook error: ... HARNESS PROTECTED PATH ..." in the
    tool output and as protected_path_blocked events in events.jsonl. This
    is why I switched to writing JSON payload fixtures via the Write tool
    (which check_lock.py governs, not guard_paths.py) before feeding them to
    guard_paths.py via stdin/redirection from Bash, to keep the test harness
    itself from tripping the now-live guard.

(3) Restore protected_paths to [] and re-prove no-op on the SAME payloads:
    state["protected_paths"] = []   (via the same guarded()/atomic_write_json
    script, run standalone — the combined restore+rm -rf one-liner I first
    tried was itself denied by the OPERATOR'S OUTER dcg guard on the
    `rm -rf` pattern, unrelated to guard_paths.py; split into the state
    restore, then `rm -f <file> && rmdir <dir>` for cleanup, which the outer
    guard allows.)
    python3 .harness/bin/guard_paths.py < redirect_payload.json  -> exit 0
    python3 .harness/bin/guard_paths.py < abs_payload.json       -> exit 0
    echo '{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"rm -f .harness/tests_protected/dummy.txt"}}' \
      | python3 .harness/bin/guard_paths.py                      -> exit 0
    python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('.harness/state.json')).get('protected_paths'))"
      -> []
    grep -c protected_path_blocked .harness/logs/events.jsonl -> 5 (unchanged
      from step 2 — confirms the restore produced ZERO new denies).

ADDITIONAL FAIL-OPEN / SCOPE CHECKS
- Non-Bash tool_name (e.g. "Edit") -> exit 0 (hook does not touch it).
- Bash tool_input with no "command" key -> exit 0.
- Malformed (non-JSON) stdin -> exit 0 (caught by the outer try/except in
  __main__, matching check_lock.py's fail-open convention exactly).

CLEANUP
- .harness/tests_protected/ and /private/tmp/guard_paths_test/ removed.
- protected_paths confirmed [] in .harness/state.json (shipped default,
  strict no-op state).
- Locks released on all three edited files (see below, prior to handoff).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA CROSSWALK (T-310.json description)
1. guard_paths.py hook, Bash-only, destructive-verb + redirect parsing,
   protected_paths glob match, deny=exit2+stderr message (check_lock.py
   convention), protected_path_blocked event on deny, fail-open on internal
   error, strict no-op with protected_paths=[] -> ALL DONE, replayed above.
2. .claude/settings.json PreToolUse[Bash] wiring without disturbing existing
   hooks -> DONE (diff is additive only, see settings.json dump above).
3. state.json protected_paths: [] added via locked CLI-adjacent script ->
   DONE (top-level key, guarded()+atomic_write_json, lock held throughout).
4. CLAUDE.md untouched -> confirmed (no edit made, no lock acquired on it).
5. Mechanical tests (1) no-op, (2) test-glob deny+event, (3) restore+re-noop
   -> all replayed above with exact commands and observed exit codes/events.

Verifier replay tip: steps (2)-(3) mutate state.json's protected_paths
in-place; if replaying, use the same harness_common.guarded()/
atomic_write_json pattern (or a temp copy) rather than hand-editing the file,
and remember protected_paths must end back at [] (verified above) since that
is the shipped default this task must not regress.

REJECTED: guard_paths.py fails the audit's own documented acceptance criterion (audit_t303_protected_paths.md ~line 160-161, "Expected measurable gain" #2): with a test glob like ["hf-space/data/**"], a `rm -rf hf-space/data` must be denied. I replayed this exact scenario myself (same glob shape, own test dir) and it is NOT denied.

REPLAY (verifier-b, independent execution, not trusting worker-c's note):
- Set protected_paths=[".harness/tests_protected_vb/**"] via hc.guarded()+atomic_write_json while holding lock.py on state.json.
- mkdir .harness/tests_protected_vb; touch dummy.txt inside it.
- CONFIRMED denies (exit 2 + protected_path_blocked event), replayed myself via synthetic stdin JSON:
  - relative file rm -f .harness/tests_protected_vb/dummy.txt -> exit 2
  - quoted-absolute file rm -f "/Users/.../Universal Harness/.harness/tests_protected_vb/dummy.txt" (repo path has a space) -> exit 2
  - redirect echo hi >> .harness/tests_protected_vb/dummy.txt -> exit 2
  - /private/tmp scratch destructive command still allowed while glob active -> exit 0 (no false positive)
  - command chaining with ; and && before the destructive verb still caught -> exit 2 both
- FAILING CASE (in-scope, no chaining/case tricks needed -- a bare recursive delete of the protected directory itself):
  command: "rm -rf .harness/tests_protected_vb" (deleting the directory itself, not a file under it)
  -> EXIT 0, ZERO protected_path_blocked event logged.
  Root cause (code review of _target_matches / fnmatch): the target ".harness/tests_protected_vb" (no trailing path segment) does not match the glob pattern ".harness/tests_protected_vb/**" under fnmatch semantics -- a glob ending in a slash + double-star requires at least one more path segment after the slash. Deleting the directory wholesale destroys everything the glob was meant to protect and uses exactly the destructive verb this hook targets; it needs zero chaining, zero case games, zero out-of-scope tooling. This is the audit's own worked example (worker used the identical dir/double-star glob shape) failing.
- Secondary, minor, recorded for the coverage boundary but NOT the rejection reason: uppercase verb form is not caught (regex is case-sensitive); arguably immaterial since real shells resolve the lowercase binary only.
- Out-of-scope bypasses tried and confirmed correctly NOT flagged (per task framing, not a failure): a python one-liner deleting a file directly via the os module, find with -delete, cp from /dev/null, truncate variants beyond the truncate verb -- all outside the audited verb list (rm|mv|shred|truncate + redirects), so passing is expected/acceptable.
- Fail-open confirmed independently: non-Bash tool_name -> exit 0; Bash with no command key -> exit 0; malformed non-JSON stdin -> exit 0.
- settings.json diff vs git HEAD: purely additive new PreToolUse matcher "Bash" block appended after the existing Edit|Write|... block; no existing hook entries touched (git diff HEAD -- .claude/settings.json).
- state.json diff vs git HEAD: exactly one new top-level key protected_paths: [] added, nothing else touched (git diff HEAD -- .harness/state.json).
- state.json restored to protected_paths=[] and md5 verified byte-identical to the pre-test snapshot (3c6b1610c6d6e0f390e533fe69daf662) both before and after my temporary glob mutation; test artifacts (.harness/tests_protected_vb/) cleaned up, none left behind.
- Locks/leases: lock.py status showed no active lock files, sweep swept 0 -- clean.

VERDICT: send back to worker-c. Fix needed: _target_matches (or the glob-authoring contract) must also match the bare directory that a dir/double-star glob is meant to protect -- e.g. also compare the target against the glob with the trailing double-star segment stripped, or explicitly require/document that operators write both "dir" and "dir/**" and update the audit's own worked example plus this task's acceptance text to match. As shipped, the hook does not deliver the audit's own stated "Expected measurable gain #2".
plan: fix _target_matches in guard_paths.py so dir/** glob also protects bare 'dir' and 'dir/' (not just files inside); re-run full mechanical proof suite + verifier's bypass case

HANDOFF -> verifier: FIX for verifier-b's REJECTED bug: _target_matches() in guard_paths.py now also protects the bare directory a `<prefix>/**` glob was written for (deleting the whole tree, the worst case), not just files strictly inside it.

CHANGE (guard_paths.py, _target_matches only):
- Candidates are normalized by stripping a trailing slash (c.rstrip("/") or c) before matching, so "dir" and "dir/" behave alike.
- In addition to the existing fnmatch(cand, glob) check, added: if glob.endswith("/**") and cand == glob[:-len("/**")]: match. This covers the bare-prefix case that fnmatch alone cannot express (fnmatch("dir", "dir/**") is False by design — the pattern requires >=1 segment after the slash).
- Existing inside-the-tree matching (fnmatch against resolved+relative-to-root candidates) is untouched.

EXPECTED vs ACTUAL: expected the verifier's exact failing command to now deny; observed exit 2 + protected_path_blocked event on first replay, confirmed below.

REPLAY (all run as worker-c, this session, in order):
1. Baseline snapshot before any test mutation:
   md5 .harness/state.json -> 3c6b1610c6d6e0f390e533fe69daf662 (matches verifier-b's recorded pre-test snapshot); protected_paths == [] confirmed via python3 -c "import json;print(json.load(open('.harness/state.json')).get('protected_paths'))".

2. Set test glob (same locked guarded()+atomic_write_json pattern as original impl, lock.py held on state.json throughout):
   protected_paths = [".harness/tests_protected_wc/**"]
   mkdir -p .harness/tests_protected_wc && touch .harness/tests_protected_wc/dummy.txt
   mkdir -p /private/tmp/guard_paths_test_wc && touch /private/tmp/guard_paths_test_wc/scratch.txt

3. Mechanical proof suite (synthetic stdin JSON payloads written to scratchpad files via the Write tool, then fed to guard_paths.py via `python3 .harness/bin/guard_paths.py < payload.json`, to avoid the outer dcg guard tripping on literal "rm -rf" text in an inline echo/heredoc):

   a. relative file: {"command":"rm -f .harness/tests_protected_wc/dummy.txt"}
      -> exit 2, event target=".harness/tests_protected_wc/dummy.txt" glob=".harness/tests_protected_wc/**" (pre-existing behavior, still works)

   b. VERIFIER'S EXACT BYPASS CASE (bare directory, no trailing segment): {"command":"rm -rf .harness/tests_protected_wc"}
      -> exit 2 (was exit 0 before this fix). stderr: "HARNESS PROTECTED PATH: command targets '.harness/tests_protected_wc', which matches protected glob '.harness/tests_protected_wc/**' ...". events.jsonl gained {"event":"protected_path_blocked","target":".harness/tests_protected_wc","glob":".harness/tests_protected_wc/**"}.

   c. trailing-slash form: {"command":"rm -rf .harness/tests_protected_wc/"}
      -> exit 2, target normalized to ".harness/tests_protected_wc/" in the log but matched via the rstrip("/") candidate.

   d. redirect form: {"command":"echo hi >> .harness/tests_protected_wc/dummy.txt"}
      -> exit 2 (regression check, unaffected by the fix).

   e. quoted absolute path with space (repo dir name has a space): {"command":"rm -f \"/Users/.../Universal Harness/.harness/tests_protected_wc/dummy.txt\""}
      -> exit 2 (regression check, unaffected by the fix).

   f. /private/tmp scratch, glob still active (selectivity / no false positive): {"command":"rm -f /private/tmp/guard_paths_test_wc/scratch.txt"}
      -> exit 0.

   grep -c protected_path_blocked .harness/logs/events.jsonl -> 22 after (a)-(e); one extra event beyond the 5 expected from (a,b,c,d,e) was logged at 16:39:10Z with a truncated command containing my own literal test-setup shell text — this is the SAME live-hook side effect the original worker noted (my own real Bash tool call got intercepted by the now-live guard because its literal command text matched the active test glob); it is additional corroboration the wiring is live, not a bug.

4. Restore + re-prove no-op (same guarded()+atomic_write_json pattern, standalone, not chained with any rm so the outer dcg guard doesn't intervene):
   protected_paths = []
   md5 .harness/state.json -> 3c6b1610c6d6e0f390e533fe69daf662 (byte-identical to step 1's baseline)
   Re-ran ALL SIX payloads (a)-(f) above against the restored empty state:
   -> ALL six: exit 0.
   grep -c protected_path_blocked .harness/logs/events.jsonl -> 22 (unchanged from step 3 -- confirms zero new denies during the no-op re-proof).

5. Cleanup: rm -f .harness/tests_protected_wc/dummy.txt && rmdir .harness/tests_protected_wc; rm -f /private/tmp/guard_paths_test_wc/scratch.txt && rmdir /private/tmp/guard_paths_test_wc. Both test dirs confirmed gone (ls -> No such file or directory).

6. Diff scope check vs git HEAD (unchanged from original impl, still additive-only):
   git diff HEAD -- .harness/state.json -> single new top-level key "protected_paths": [] added, nothing else touched.
   git diff HEAD -- .claude/settings.json -> single new PreToolUse "Bash" matcher block appended after the existing Edit|Write|... block, no existing hooks touched.
   guard_paths.py is untracked (new file), diff is the _target_matches fix described above (git diff not meaningful since untracked; full current file content is the artifact).

CLAUDE.md: not touched (still out of scope per task text).

Locks: acquired+released on .harness/bin/guard_paths.py and .harness/state.json for this session (lock.py acquire/release, holder worker-c). No lock files left behind.

Verifier replay tip: to reproduce step 3(b) directly, set protected_paths=[".harness/tests_protected_wc/**"] (or any dir/** glob), mkdir the dir, then feed {"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"rm -rf <dir>"}} to guard_paths.py via stdin -- expect exit 2 and a protected_path_blocked event with target==<dir> (bare, no trailing slash). Restore protected_paths=[] afterward; md5 of state.json should return to 3c6b1610c6d6e0f390e533fe69daf662.

VERIFIED: worker-c's fix to _target_matches closes my exact bypass. Independent replay (verifier-b, synthetic stdin payloads via file redirection, avoiding literal destructive text in own Bash tool-call text): (1) Set protected_paths=['.harness/tests_protected_vb/**'] via hc.guarded()+atomic_write_json while holding lock.py on state.json. Bare-dir bypass 'rm -rf .harness/tests_protected_vb' -> exit 2 + protected_path_blocked event (target='.harness/tests_protected_vb', glob='.harness/tests_protected_vb/**') -- was exit 0/no event before the fix. Trailing-slash form 'rm -rf .harness/tests_protected_vb/' -> exit 2 + event, target normalized via rstrip. (2) Regression sweep, all against the same active glob: file-inside-tree rm -f .../dummy.txt -> exit 2; redirect echo hi >> .../dummy.txt -> exit 2; quoted-absolute-with-space rm -f "/Users/.../Universal Harness/.../dummy.txt" -> exit 2 (repo path space handled); /private/tmp scratch rm -f -> exit 0 (no false positive, selectivity preserved). (3) Cognitive-diversity checks beyond the producer's note: near-miss directory names '.harness/tests_protected_vb2' and '.harness/tests_protected_vb-other' (rm -rf) both -> exit 0 -- confirms the new 'cand == glob[:-len("/**")]' check is an exact-equality match, not a prefix match, so the fix does not widen matching beyond the bare-prefix case; fail-open spot checks (non-Bash tool_name, Bash with no command key, malformed non-JSON stdin) all -> exit 0, consistent with check_lock.py convention; static read of _target_matches confirmed the diff is minimal -- one candidate-normalization rstrip plus one explicit bare-prefix equality branch, nothing else touched. (4) Restore: protected_paths set back to [] via the same guarded()/atomic_write_json pattern; state.json md5 3c6b1610c6d6e0f390e533fe69daf662 verified identical before my test mutation, after restore, and matches the pre-existing baseline recorded in the prior REJECTED note. Re-ran all 8 payloads (bare-dir, trailing-slash, inside-file, redirect, quoted-abs, tmp-noop, both near-misses) against the restored empty state -> all exit 0, zero new protected_path_blocked events (event count held at 27 before and after the no-op re-proof). Test artifacts (.harness/tests_protected_vb/, /private/tmp/guard_paths_test_vb2/) removed and confirmed gone. Locks: acquired+released cleanly on state.json (holder verifier-b); lock.py status shows no active lock files, sweep swept 0. git diff HEAD -- .claude/settings.json and .harness/state.json confirmed still additive-only (unchanged from prior round). Verdict: bypass is dead, no regressions, no widened matching. Accepting.
